public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quota scribe.
manner: Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a quota scribe still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quota scribe in and around Guildheart Hub, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Guildheart Hub workable through the obligations of quota scribe while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Guildheart Hub, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of quota scribe visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Mira Ledgermere can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.
Mira Ledgermere can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Mira Ledgermere communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Guildheart Hub: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
